mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, reducing expected future earnings and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling window or positive returns dominate during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, labor market tightness triggers wage inflation expectations, prompting markets to price in tighter monetary policy and economic slowdown, resulting in negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals a broad-based demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2% quarter-over-quarter, supply-side expansion supports corporate output and profit margins, generating positive forward equity returns unless offset by monetary tightening.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative or flat forward returns in early-cycle regimes over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.0%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank rate hikes to restore price stability, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next two weeks.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite high inflation readings.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a stable or rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation deterioration, signaling underlying economic weakness that suppresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which directly supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production contracts for two consecutive months, it signals an imminent supply-side recession that forces earnings downgrades, leading to sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next two weeks.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines do not precede negative returns in >55% of historical instances.
